author: aashritha
message:
Use Postgres as the primary datastore for ShiftLog

Evaluated SQLite vs Postgres for shift records. Going with Postgres from day one because we'll need concurrent writes once multiple managers are editing schedules simultaneously, and SQLite's single-writer lock would bite us fast.
diff_summary: Added SQLAlchemy engine config pointing at Postgres; added Shift, Employee, Manager models.

author: aashritha
message:
Adopt FastAPI for the backend API

Chose FastAPI over Flask for built-in async support and automatic OpenAPI docs, which we'll want for the mobile client later.
diff_summary: Scaffolded FastAPI app with /health endpoint; removed Flask dependency.

author: aashritha
message:
Fix double-booking bug in shift assignment

Bug: two managers could assign the same employee to overlapping shifts because the overlap check ran after the INSERT instead of before it, inside a race window.
Fix: wrapped the overlap check and insert in a single DB transaction with a SELECT ... FOR UPDATE row lock on the employee's shift range.
diff_summary: Moved overlap validation inside the transaction, added row-level lock.

author: aashritha
message:
Use JWT (not server-side sessions) for auth

Stateless JWT chosen so the API can scale horizontally without sticky sessions or a shared session store. Refresh tokens stored in httpOnly cookies.

author: aashritha
message:
Revert "Use Redis pub/sub for real-time shift-change notifications"

This reverts commit 9f1a220. Redis pub/sub dropped messages silently when a client reconnected during a deploy, and debugging that on the free-tier Redis instance (no persistence, no cluster) wasn't worth it for our current scale. Switched to a 10-second client-side poll on /shifts/updates instead — simpler, and the extra latency doesn't matter for a scheduling app.
diff_summary: Removed Redis pub/sub notifier; added simple polling endpoint with last-modified timestamp.

author: aashritha
message:
Fix timezone bug in shift start/end times

Bug: shift times were stored and compared as naive datetimes, so a manager in EST creating a shift showed up 5 hours off for an employee viewing in UTC.
Fix: store all shift timestamps as UTC-aware datetimes in Postgres (timestamptz) and convert to the viewer's timezone only at the API response layer.
diff_summary: Changed Shift.start_time/end_time columns to TIMESTAMPTZ; added timezone conversion in response serializer.

author: aashritha
message:
Rate-limit the /shifts/updates poll endpoint

Added per-user rate limiting (1 req/8s) after load testing showed the naive 10s client poll could still hammer the endpoint if a browser tab retried aggressively on error.
diff_summary: Added token-bucket rate limiter middleware scoped to authenticated user id.

author: aashritha
message:
Fix N+1 query on manager dashboard

Bug: /dashboard/summary was issuing one query per employee to fetch their shift count, causing 3-4s load times for teams over 30 people.
Fix: replaced the per-employee loop with a single GROUP BY query using SQLAlchemy's joinedload/aggregate.
diff_summary: Replaced N+1 loop with a single aggregate query; dashboard load dropped from ~3.8s to ~180ms.

author: aashritha
message:
Move Postgres from local Docker to Railway-managed Postgres

Supersedes the local-Docker Postgres setup from a1f3c02. Local Docker Postgres was fine for solo dev but broke onboarding for a second contributor (schema drift, no shared instance). Moved to a single Railway-managed Postgres instance that both dev and staging point at, with migrations run through Alembic on deploy.
diff_summary: Swapped local DB connection string for Railway DATABASE_URL env var; added Alembic migration scaffold.

author: aashritha
message:
Add optimistic UI updates on the shift calendar

Decided to update the calendar view immediately on drag-and-drop reschedule, then reconcile with the server response, rather than waiting for the round-trip — the 10s poll made drag-and-drop feel laggy without it.
diff_summary: Added optimistic local state update with rollback-on-error handling.

author: aashritha
message:
Fix optimistic update rollback not clearing stale shift on error

Bug: when the server rejected a reschedule (e.g. overlap), the optimistic UI update wasn't rolled back correctly — the shift stayed in the wrong slot until a manual refresh.
Fix: rollback now re-fetches the single affected shift instead of trying to diff the optimistic state manually.
diff_summary: Replaced manual state diff rollback with a targeted re-fetch of the affected shift on error.

author: aashritha
message:
Remove legacy CSV export feature

Dropped the CSV export endpoint and UI button — nobody used it after we shipped the PDF export in 3d0f5a2's follow-up, and it was the only thing still depending on the old pandas dependency.
diff_summary: Deleted csv_export.py and CsvExportButton.tsx; removed pandas from requirements.txt.

author: aashritha
message:
Fix rate limiter false-positive on shared office IP

Bug: rate limiting was accidentally keyed by IP for unauthenticated requests, so an entire office behind one NAT'd IP got throttled together.
Fix: keyed unauthenticated rate limiting by a per-session cookie id instead of raw IP; authenticated requests remain keyed by user id.
diff_summary: Changed rate-limit key derivation for unauthenticated requests from request.client.host to a signed session cookie id.

author: aashritha
message:
Adopt Alembic autogenerate for all future schema changes

Formalizing what 2c9e4f1 started — every schema change from now on must go through an Alembic migration, no more hand-edited SQL against the Railway instance. Added a pre-commit check that fails if models.py changed without a matching migration file.
diff_summary: Configured Alembic autogenerate against SQLAlchemy metadata; added pre-commit hook enforcing migration-model parity.

author: aashritha
message:
Move auth from JWT to server-side session cookies

Supersedes the JWT approach from d5b6e13. JWT refresh-token rotation was causing logout bugs on mobile Safari (ITP silently dropping the httpOnly cookie), and since ShiftLog's API and frontend are same-origin behind one Railway service, the horizontal-scaling argument for JWT never actually applied. Switched to server-side sessions backed by the Railway Postgres instance (a plain session table, not a pub/sub cache) for simpler, more reliable auth.
diff_summary: Replaced JWT issuing/verification with server-side session table + signed session cookie; removed jwt_handler.py.

author: aashritha
message:
Add pagination to the /shifts list endpoint

Switched /shifts from returning the full result set to cursor-based pagination (created_at + id as the cursor), since a manager's shift history was starting to return several hundred rows on a single request. No downstream consumer depended on the unpaginated response shape yet, so this was a clean cut instead of a versioned breaking change.
diff_summary: Added cursor-based pagination to GET /shifts; response now returns {items, next_cursor}.
